transcript.text: And I would have ever anticipated.
This meeting is being recorded.
We're dealing with, but yeah, aside from that, it's, I mean, we're selling product, which is great.
Nice.
People that don't know us are buying it, so.
Amazing.
So far, so good.
Yeah, you're doing something right if folks are finding you right out the gate.
Yeah.
So, cool, cool.
Well, I imagine we could both use as much time as we can finagle, so I'll kind of blast through some of this, and we can dive into any particulars that you want, but quick update.

We did get 10 of those articles re-link checked, and they've been checked off in that spreadsheet that we were working out of, and the five of the articles that got passed back will be to you by the end of this week, which means, well, to clarify, we'll have them, like all those revisions applied, and then we send them to our publishing team, who will get them staged next week, and I'll send you over those links.
Because I know you do like image QC and things.
Speaking of, I don't know if there's a point at which would be helpful outside of like, you know, if you have images like pre-selected and share those with us.
Like I could potentially add a section to like the Airtable where you could just upload it.
And then our team could incorporate those images so that you're not having to go in every single time.
I'm just thinking through like how I can help lighten your load on the back end.
And if there's any other like specific QC things that you're adapting to that I could connect with our publishing team on doing.
Yeah, I don't know if there's anything top of your mind right now, but something to keep in mind going forward.
Yeah, on the image side, it's, it would be helpful if you guys did it.
But it's not like a deal breaker just because basically like Liz with the co-founder, she's the one that's actually doing the image selection.
And so I have like a separate type.
Where she's like pre-fetched a bunch of images.
So in some cases, like I already have something that I can just, I could actually add it to the Airtable.
But it's also, usually she hasn't done it.
And so it's like, hey, once it's been staging, it's like, okay, great.
Like here it is.
Can you just grab an image for me?
So, and it doesn't take very long to upload it.
So it's, for now, it's fine with the current process.
I'll let you know if that changes.
Awesome.
Yeah.
I just like thinking through, you know, big picture, like looking ahead, figure that one might be.
A potentially like easy pain point for us to lift.
Other things.
I do think, just going back to the publishing, there's kind of two things that could be helpful.
One, right now when you guys publish, it comes in as GrowthX.
If you can just change it to Joey Zellinger.
We're not disclosing.
We're hiding on the site the author.
But in the event that like, for some reason it were to display, we would just rather it
Joey versus you guys.
That's one thing.
Another thing is there's the featured product section.
If they can just make sure whichever product is called out in the talents just to select that product, that would be great.
So I just don't have to do it.
And then I'd say the last thing is we have – I could share that with you, but there's the ability to tag the content.
And so at the very least, this doesn't have to be the case for every article because some articles might have a different tag.
But, like, if it's talking about your reproductive years, just tag it as reproductive years.
Perimenopause, same thing.
And postmenopause, same thing.
Okay.
I'll also go in because there's additional tags we're going to use for, like, curating the blog.
But that would just be – it just would be helpful.
Yeah, that makes sense.
Is it just those three tags that you're primarily –
Are there others that we should consider?
There's others too, but I don't like, until we have enough like critical mass, it's hard to like use the tag and then figure out how we're going to curate it, I guess.
So for now, those are really the three.
Okay, cool.
What I'll probably do is add, I'll probably add a column to Airtable so that we like specifically flag that just for like additional visibility for my publishing team.
That shouldn't be a huge lift on our NMI take like end of next week to get that implemented.
It's fine, yeah.
Awesome.
Yeah, no, that's all good to know.
With the, for the featured product, is that a tag or is there?
There's like a section.
So like when they're, it's below, I think it's literally the CMS.
It's below the key take away section.
There's like a feature.
And you can actually add all, like, multiple products there.
But usually, I feel like most articles tend to, like, only feature one.
The only use case would be, like, if an ingredient is across the products, like, you know, bacillus coagulans is across all three.
So you could tag all three.
Or if it's, like, vitamin A or something, you could tag all three.
But for the most part, the articles tend to be more, like, age-specific.
you just add more granular.
Yeah, that makes sense.
Cool.
I will work on that.
Worst case, if need any clarification, I'll just, I might, like, pull together a little document of, like, how we're understanding how to do that.
And how do we just put quick eyes on it before we start sorting everything?
Well, cool.
Other general updates for me to move on.
I wanted to quickly follow up on those two articles that I had flagged in Slack.
There was the one, one, one where I'm thinking
About like cramps in general and magnesium, I believe.
And then the other one where we had added a few new sections and wanted to check and see if that needed to go back through review just so we put in the right status for you guys.
Do you have any insight on those?
I don't.
I'll follow up with Maggie after this.
But yeah, I messaged her, but I haven't heard back.
Okay.
No worries.
I guess I know it's a crazy week.
Um, and then other things coming up.
I have it on my laptop and on my other main screen, so I don't know where to look.
Um, we thank you for the GA4 and GSC access, um, got our team working on setting up a looker dashboard, which basically helps us.
Like we'll split out that data.
Um, and then we'll also look specifically at like GrowthX content, um, as we're doing some reporting.
So we'll start to do some initial reporting in January, um, like just kind of frame expectations or like performance.
Yeah.
Usually, it takes about three months to see, like, really consistent growth across content, across websites, especially when it's really new.
And then at, like, the six-month mark is when you would see things, like, really start to take off with consistent publication.
So we'll probably see, like, initial spike just because the site didn't exist before.
But the first, like, month or so is really going to be getting a baseline on things and then kind of watching for any trends.

Yeah, and if there's anything, like, as you stand up the analytics, like, if your team, I don't know if you have, like, any technical, like, SEO folks on staff, but if they're going through the site and they're like, hey, like, we recommend doing X, Y, or Z, like, just flag those things and let us know.
Cool.
Yeah.
As they set up, I'll definitely, if they do flag anything, I'll ping you.
And I know we do have some technical SEO folks that would sometimes, like, look through some content.
Um, so I can potentially follow up on that.
Um, otherwise...
So for the pipeline, that like the custom update for that like Gemini prompt and things that we're talking about, I followed up, it is in the queue for our engineering team to work on.
With holidays, there's a little bit of a backlog.
So I'm to follow up again and see if we can get that prioritized and if I can get some clarity on like exactly when they're going to be able to jump into that.
Because I know that's a big piece that's taking a lot of your guys' time on the review end.
And so hopefully next week, I'll have a little bit clearer timeline on when that can be more thoroughly implemented.

And then I was able to start to dig into that content strategy that you had mentioned, you and Matthew had talked about for like some top of funnel, sort of more general content, not linked to specific products.
I'm going to dig into that plan a little bit more.
I've actually got it up right here.
And we...
I've done some initial keyword research around topics in this initial proposal.
So far, the keyword research is fairly surface level, so I need to dig into it a little bit more.
Timing on that, I can probably have a more robust set of assignment ideas for us to look at by January.

And we will need to develop a slightly different pipeline for that content, just since it's going to go through, you know, like linking and things might be a little bit different, like MD tags and everything else.
So once we get the assignments, we'll also start to put together a test pipeline, and then we can send over some calibration articles.
That would all probably happen early January.
Yeah, sounds good.
Let's see here.
Other things in my mind.
Oh, yeah, holidays.
Anyways, I'm looking at, just, you know, figured that a good to lay out kind of roadmap for what we got the next couple weeks.
Myself and most of our colleagues.
The production team are out Christmas and New Year's, and we have an organization-wide quiet week, the week of New Year's, so that there's no new content coming out that week in particular.
But next week, what we're going to do is we're going to basically deliver double a typical week's worth of content.
So we'll have 10 new articles coming to you next week.
Some of those were in progress already, which is why we postponed the link checking slightly.
So 10 net new articles, and we'll get two to three of those additional pending revisions that are currently with us right now.
think there's nine, including the five that we're sending to you this week.
So we'll still have a batch of new content going to publication by the end of this week and by the end of next week with those revisions complete.
Great.
Also means that we're not going to be meeting for a couple weeks, so we'll meet next week if you're available.
And then...
The week of Christmas and New Year's will be out, and so we'll regroup that first week of January.
Yeah, sounds good.
It'll be nice to get some time off.
We'll be grinding over here, but yeah, sounds good.
Awesome.
I will be on Slack lightly.
I'm traveling, flying back to see my folks, so there's some days that I might be very lightly available, but if there's any major concerns, feel free to ping us.
I'll be on most days with the exception of Christmas and New Year's and able to respond a little bit and follow up on some stuff.
Sounds good.
So, yeah, we're not completely abandoning you guys.
Yeah, sounds good.
We'll start flagging those articles that went through the link checker to Maggie, and yeah, let's take them from there.
Awesome.
Yeah, and I know we only chipped away at like 10 of them.
We'll continue like really chipping away at that at the new year as well once.
Yeah.
Content Production Team is back in full swing.
Anything else on your radar for this week and next?
No, we're just trying to just keep the ball rolling right now.
Keep your head above water.
No, I feel that.
Cool.
Well, if there's anything that comes up, yeah, feel free to ping me.
I'll see what I can do to help out.
Okay, sounds good.
Thanks.
Yeah, bye.
Bye.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Sync on content production, publishing workflow, and upcoming strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/vkLj-2xRFD1bX_oXK6pTBhaxCYV1PqMR?tab=summary&timestamp=23.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Publishing Workflow Streamlined:** GrowthX will now handle three tasks previously done by Biologica: setting the author to "Joey Zellinger," selecting the featured product, and applying key tags (`reproductive years`, `perimenopause`, `postmenopause`).](https://fathom.video/share/vkLj-2xRFD1bX_oXK6pTBhaxCYV1PqMR?tab=summary&timestamp=155.0)
  - [**Production Accelerated for Holidays:** To front-load work before the holiday break, GrowthX will deliver 10 new articles next week, plus 2–3 revised ones.](https://fathom.video/share/vkLj-2xRFD1bX_oXK6pTBhaxCYV1PqMR?tab=summary&timestamp=591.0)
  - [**New Content Strategy in Progress:** A plan for top-of-funnel content is being developed, with initial assignments expected in January. This will require a new, distinct production pipeline.](https://fathom.video/share/vkLj-2xRFD1bX_oXK6pTBhaxCYV1PqMR?tab=summary&timestamp=519.0)
  - [**Analytics Dashboard Launching:** GrowthX is building a Looker dashboard to track content performance using GA4/GSC data, with initial reporting to begin in January.](https://fathom.video/share/vkLj-2xRFD1bX_oXK6pTBhaxCYV1PqMR?tab=summary&timestamp=395.0)

## Topics

### Publishing Workflow Optimization

  - [To reduce Biologica's workload, GrowthX will now manage three tasks during publication:](https://fathom.video/share/vkLj-2xRFD1bX_oXK6pTBhaxCYV1PqMR?tab=summary&timestamp=155.0)
      - [**Author:** Set to "Joey Zellinger" (prevents "GrowthX" from appearing if author is ever displayed).](https://fathom.video/share/vkLj-2xRFD1bX_oXK6pTBhaxCYV1PqMR?tab=summary&timestamp=155.0)
      - [**Featured Product:** Select the product(s) mentioned in the article's text.](https://fathom.video/share/vkLj-2xRFD1bX_oXK6pTBhaxCYV1PqMR?tab=summary&timestamp=184.0)
      - [**Tags:** Apply key tags for content curation: `reproductive years`, `perimenopause`, `postmenopause`.](https://fathom.video/share/vkLj-2xRFD1bX_oXK6pTBhaxCYV1PqMR?tab=summary&timestamp=202.0)
          - [GrowthX will add a tag column to Airtable to ensure visibility for the publishing team.](https://fathom.video/share/vkLj-2xRFD1bX_oXK6pTBhaxCYV1PqMR?tab=summary&timestamp=202.0)
  - [**Image QC Process:** The current process (Liz selects images post-staging) will continue, as it is not a significant bottleneck.](https://fathom.video/share/vkLj-2xRFD1bX_oXK6pTBhaxCYV1PqMR?tab=summary&timestamp=102.0)

### Content Production & Strategy

  - [**Link Checking:** 10 articles are complete; 5 revised articles will be delivered by EOW.](https://fathom.video/share/vkLj-2xRFD1bX_oXK6pTBhaxCYV1PqMR?tab=summary&timestamp=23.0)
  - [**Pending Reviews:** Brian will follow up with Maggie on two articles flagged in Slack (one on cramps/magnesium, one with new sections) to determine if they need re-review.](https://fathom.video/share/vkLj-2xRFD1bX_oXK6pTBhaxCYV1PqMR?tab=summary&timestamp=353.0)
  - [**Top-of-Funnel Strategy:**](https://fathom.video/share/vkLj-2xRFD1bX_oXK6pTBhaxCYV1PqMR?tab=summary&timestamp=519.0)
      - [GrowthX is developing a plan for general, non-product-specific content.](https://fathom.video/share/vkLj-2xRFD1bX_oXK6pTBhaxCYV1PqMR?tab=summary&timestamp=519.0)
      - [Initial keyword research is complete; a robust set of assignment ideas is expected in January.](https://fathom.video/share/vkLj-2xRFD1bX_oXK6pTBhaxCYV1PqMR?tab=summary&timestamp=519.0)
      - [A new production pipeline will be built for this content, followed by calibration articles.](https://fathom.video/share/vkLj-2xRFD1bX_oXK6pTBhaxCYV1PqMR?tab=summary&timestamp=562.0)
  - [**Gemini Prompt Customization:** The request is in the engineering queue. Katya will push for prioritization and a timeline, as this is a major bottleneck for Biologica's review process.](https://fathom.video/share/vkLj-2xRFD1bX_oXK6pTBhaxCYV1PqMR?tab=summary&timestamp=480.0)

### Analytics & Reporting

  - [GrowthX has GA4/GSC access and is building a Looker dashboard to track content performance.](https://fathom.video/share/vkLj-2xRFD1bX_oXK6pTBhaxCYV1PqMR?tab=summary&timestamp=395.0)
  - [Initial reporting will begin in January.](https://fathom.video/share/vkLj-2xRFD1bX_oXK6pTBhaxCYV1PqMR?tab=summary&timestamp=395.0)
  - [**Performance Expectations:**](https://fathom.video/share/vkLj-2xRFD1bX_oXK6pTBhaxCYV1PqMR?tab=summary&timestamp=411.0)
      - [**Baseline:** First month will establish a baseline.](https://fathom.video/share/vkLj-2xRFD1bX_oXK6pTBhaxCYV1PqMR?tab=summary&timestamp=411.0)
      - [**Growth:** Consistent growth expected at the 3-month mark.](https://fathom.video/share/vkLj-2xRFD1bX_oXK6pTBhaxCYV1PqMR?tab=summary&timestamp=411.0)
      - [**Take-off:** Significant growth expected at the 6-month mark.](https://fathom.video/share/vkLj-2xRFD1bX_oXK6pTBhaxCYV1PqMR?tab=summary&timestamp=411.0)
  - [**Technical SEO:** GrowthX will flag any technical SEO recommendations to Brian as their team reviews the site.](https://fathom.video/share/vkLj-2xRFD1bX_oXK6pTBhaxCYV1PqMR?tab=summary&timestamp=445.0)

### Holiday Schedule

  - [**Next Week (Dec 15–19):**](https://fathom.video/share/vkLj-2xRFD1bX_oXK6pTBhaxCYV1PqMR?tab=summary&timestamp=591.0)
      - [**Deliverables:** 10 new articles + 2–3 revised articles.](https://fathom.video/share/vkLj-2xRFD1bX_oXK6pTBhaxCYV1PqMR?tab=summary&timestamp=613.0)
      - [**Meetings:** The weekly sync will occur.](https://fathom.video/share/vkLj-2xRFD1bX_oXK6pTBhaxCYV1PqMR?tab=summary&timestamp=653.0)
  - [**Dec 22–Jan 2:**](https://fathom.video/share/vkLj-2xRFD1bX_oXK6pTBhaxCYV1PqMR?tab=summary&timestamp=589.0)
      - [GrowthX team is out of office.](https://fathom.video/share/vkLj-2xRFD1bX_oXK6pTBhaxCYV1PqMR?tab=summary&timestamp=589.0)
      - [No new content will be published during the "quiet week" of Jan 1.](https://fathom.video/share/vkLj-2xRFD1bX_oXK6pTBhaxCYV1PqMR?tab=summary&timestamp=589.0)
  - [**Availability:** Katya will be lightly available on Slack for urgent issues.](https://fathom.video/share/vkLj-2xRFD1bX_oXK6pTBhaxCYV1PqMR?tab=summary&timestamp=676.0)
  - [**Next Sync:** The first week of January.](https://fathom.video/share/vkLj-2xRFD1bX_oXK6pTBhaxCYV1PqMR?tab=summary&timestamp=653.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Katya:**](https://fathom.video/share/vkLj-2xRFD1bX_oXK6pTBhaxCYV1PqMR?tab=summary&timestamp=23.0)
      - [Deliver 5 revised articles by EOW.](https://fathom.video/share/vkLj-2xRFD1bX_oXK6pTBhaxCYV1PqMR?tab=summary&timestamp=36.0)
      - [Deliver 10 new + 2–3 revised articles next week.](https://fathom.video/share/vkLj-2xRFD1bX_oXK6pTBhaxCYV1PqMR?tab=summary&timestamp=613.0)
      - [Implement publishing workflow changes (author, product, tags) by end of next week.](https://fathom.video/share/vkLj-2xRFD1bX_oXK6pTBhaxCYV1PqMR?tab=summary&timestamp=155.0)
      - [Push engineering for a timeline on the Gemini prompt customization.](https://fathom.video/share/vkLj-2xRFD1bX_oXK6pTBhaxCYV1PqMR?tab=summary&timestamp=480.0)
      - [Deliver top-of-funnel content assignments and pipeline plan in January.](https://fathom.video/share/vkLj-2xRFD1bX_oXK6pTBhaxCYV1PqMR?tab=summary&timestamp=519.0)
  - [**Brian:**](https://fathom.video/share/vkLj-2xRFD1bX_oXK6pTBhaxCYV1PqMR?tab=summary&timestamp=353.0)
      - [Follow up with Maggie on the two pending articles flagged in Slack.](https://fathom.video/share/vkLj-2xRFD1bX_oXK6pTBhaxCYV1PqMR?tab=summary&timestamp=353.0)
      - [Flag articles that have completed link checking for Maggie's review.](https://fathom.video/share/vkLj-2xRFD1bX_oXK6pTBhaxCYV1PqMR?tab=summary&timestamp=702.0)

action_items.description: Update publishing workflow: set author Joey Zellinger; add featured product per article; tag Reproductive Years/Perimenopause/Postmenopause
Add tag column to Airtable for publishing team
Follow up w/ Maggie re: 2 articles flagged in Slack; confirm if review needed
Follow up w/ eng on Gemini prompt; share timeline w/ Brian
Deliver 10 new articles next week; send staged links to Brian
